mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes using custom resources. It is ideal for users who want a Kubernetes-native way to manage Prometheus and related components, but it requires understanding of Kubernetes CRDs. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Prometheus Operator Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Prometheus Operator.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for the Prometheus Operator to function properly, as they define the custom resources it will manage.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/main/bundle.yaml

```

> This command installs all required CRDs.

steps.title: Install the kube-prometheus-stack
steps.description:
This step installs the kube-prometheus-stack, which includes Prometheus, Alertmanager, and Grafana.

```bash

helm install prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --version 45.0.0 --namespace monitoring --create-namespace

```

> Replace '45.0.0' with the desired version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks that the Prometheus Operator and its components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

kubectl get svc -n monitoring

```

> You should see pods for Prometheus, Alertmanager, and Grafana.
> Check that the services are created and running.

steps.title: Access Grafana
steps.description:
This step provides instructions to access the Grafana dashboard for monitoring.

```bash

kubectl port-forward svc/prometheus-grafana -n monitoring 3000:80

echo 'Access Grafana at http://localhost:3000'

```

> This command forwards port 3000 on your local machine to Grafana.
> Use this URL to access the Grafana dashboard.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the kube-prometheus-stack
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Prometheus Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all monitoring data.

```bash

helm uninstall prometheus --namespace monitoring

kubectl delete namespace monitoring

```

> This command uninstalls the kube-prometheus-stack.
> This command deletes the monitoring namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the kube-prometheus-stack
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Prometheus data. This step will upgrade the stack to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp prometheus-prometheus-0:/prometheus /tmp/prometheus-backup

helm upgrade prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --version 45.0.0 --namespace monitoring

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Backup Prometheus data.
> Replace '45.0.0' with the desired version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Prometheus" in version "monitoring.coreos.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CRDs were not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/main/bundle.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 10.0.0.1:443: i/o timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This suggests that your Kubernetes cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure your cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana dashboard shows 'Data source not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Prometheus data source is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Prometheus service is running and configured in Grafana.

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus pod is in CrashLoopBackOff state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <prometheus-pod-name> -n monitoring' and adjust resource limits if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Prometheus API features, improved Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy configurations in favor of new CRD specifications.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.76 or earlier, review the new CRD specifications and adjust your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see all Prometheus, Alertmanager, and Grafana pods running in the 'monitoring' namespace with 'Running' status when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
